title: Classical Ciphers and Cryptanalysis as Defect Labs · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed security review reconstructs classical ciphers and cryptanalysis as defect labs from threat, algorithm, parameter, implementation, key-custody, failure, test-vector, operations, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one retained misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for classical cipher attack and migration dossier after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Implement and break a bounded shift or substitution cipher to connect keyspace, language redundancy, frequency evidence, and attacker cost.
Demonstrate how repeated XOR keystream use reveals relationships between plaintexts without presenting XOR alone as production encryption.
Explain one-time-pad key length, uniformity, secrecy, single use, distribution, and destruction requirements and their operational cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Crypto Go Legacy Migration Boundary
steps.instruction:
review case — A review cryptographic engineering team handles case 2115 during a backup restoration drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to model an authorized classical-cipher attack while quarantining all legacy use to migration or defect labs; cap bytes, work, memory, time, attempts, concurrency, and retained secret material before use; then double attacker-controlled size or work and remain inside the admission budget. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Quarantine toy and obsolete algorithms behind fixtures, negative tests, decryption-only migration, telemetry, deadlines, and deletion criteria.

Inspect the Crypto Go Legacy Migration Boundary trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Cryptography and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Crypto Go Legacy Migration Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for quarantine toy and obsolete algorithms behind fixtures, negative tests, decryption-only migration, telemetry, deadlines, and deletion criteria. For Cryptography and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Crypto Go Legacy Migration Boundary, keep Crypto Go Legacy Migration Boundary connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

Competency probe: Quarantine toy and obsolete algorithms behind fixtures, negative tests, decryption-only migration, telemetry, deadlines, and deletion criteria.

Evidence target: Cryptography and Go Record what required effort · Review Crypto Go Legacy Migration Boundary.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Cryptography and Go Record what required effort · Review Crypto Go Legacy Migration Boundary, keep Crypto Go Legacy Migration Boundary connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Quarantine toy and obsolete algorithms behind fixtures, negative tests, decryption-only migration, telemetry, deadlines, and deletion criteria.

hints: For Cryptography and Go Record what required effort · Review Crypto Go Legacy Migration Boundary, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Crypto Go Legacy Migration Boundary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Cryptography and Go Record what required effort · Review Crypto Go Legacy Migration Boundary, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review cryptographic engineering team handles case 5769 during a backup restoration drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to model an authorized classical-cipher attack while quarantining all legacy use to migration or defect labs; record key custody, terminal failure, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk ownership; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Quarantine toy and obsolete algorithms behind fixtures, negative tests, decryption-only migration, telemetry, deadlines, and deletion criteria.

For Cryptography and Go Record what required effort · Review Crypto Go Legacy Migration Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Legacy cryptography can remain available indefinitely if marked deprecated.
